subject: Anthropology
title: Language Acquisition
description: Learn Language Acquisition in one minute.

hook.text: No one teaches babies grammar rules. Yet by age four, every child speaks in full sentences. How?

buildup.text: Children acquire language through immersion, not formal instruction. They absorb patterns from speech around them.

discovery.text: Noam Chomsky proposed a universal grammar — an innate brain structure that makes language learning possible.

twist.text: Anthropologists argue social interaction, not biology alone, drives acquisition. Kids learn language by using it.

climax.text: Language acquisition blends biology and culture. The brain is wired for language, but culture provides the content.

punchline.text: Nature gives the capacity; culture fills it.

quiz.question: What did Chomsky propose about language?
options: Humans have an innate capacity for grammar
